synthesis.summary: Eaton dropped 3.1% on elevated volume following Q1 earnings, which the market received negatively — the report failed to satisfy expectations sufficiently to sustain the stock's recent level. Volume running 2.56x normal confirms the selloff is earnings-driven, though the medium-impact classification of the news suggests the results weren't catastrophically bad, pointing to guidance or margins as the likely pressure point rather than an outright miss.

FISV dropped 9.6% on 2.3x normal volume with no company-specific news in the supplied feed to account for the move. The only available news covers broad market strength (Nasdaq 100, Intel), which runs counter to FISV's sharp selloff, suggesting an idiosyncratic catalyst — likely an earnings miss, guidance cut, or large options/institutional flow — is driving this but is not captured in the provided news.

No company-specific news in the supplied set explains MU's 11.5% surge. The broader Nasdaq 100 breaking 28,000 provides macro tailwind context, but a broad index move doesn't account for MU's outsized single-day gain or its 27% 5-day run. The move is likely driven by factors not visible here — earnings expectations, options flow, or sector rotation into memory/semis.

PLTR dropped 7.3% on elevated volume following Q1 earnings, with multiple analysts revising their price targets in the aftermath. The analyst revisions provide context for the selloff but don't fully explain the magnitude — the scale of the drop suggests the earnings report itself disappointed expectations or the post-earnings reset in valuation drove heavier-than-usual selling.

Multiple analysts cut their price targets on Paramount Skydance following Q1 results, signaling that earnings disappointed expectations and prompting a selloff. The -3.1% drop is consistent with post-earnings estimate revisions, though the relatively low relative volume (0.77) suggests the move isn't outsized in terms of participation.

SNDK is up 12.4% today and 40.8% over five days, but none of the supplied news items explain this move — the available headlines cover broad market activity and generic options flow alerts with no SNDK-specific catalyst. The overbought RSI of 81.5 and relatively modest volume (1.15x average) suggest this could be driven by options flow, short covering, or a catalyst not captured in the provided news.

SHOP is down nearly 17% on 3.5x normal volume, a move consistent with a significant earnings miss, guidance cut, or other major negative disclosure — none of which are present in the supplied news. The only available news item is a positive product milestone (hitting $100B GMV), which does not explain a sell-off of this magnitude or direction.

Applied Digital announced a spin-off of its cloud business alongside a $300 million funding raise, which investors are pricing as a significant value unlock — the structural separation of a capital-intensive cloud unit plus a large cash injection directly justifies the ~11% single-day surge and the broader 5-day run.

The 5-day gain of ~24% and bullish MACD cross suggest momentum-driven buying, but the catalyst — whether options flow, sector rotation into crypto-adjacent names, or an undiscovered headline — is not visible here.

The news confirms a fundamental demand-side deterioration rather than a company-specific operational miss, which partially explains the magnitude given broader sector softness.
